title: androstoma verticillatum
intro: Androstoma verticillatum, also known as the whorled beardtongue or whorled penstemon, is a perennial plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is native to the eastern and central regions of North America, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Androstoma verticillatum is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
